description: Two entries whose sections carry the close flow's causal proposal labels verbatim (Root cause (proposal), Contributing factors (proposals), Action items (proposals)), plus a top-level 'sections' key: the ordered [label, block] pairs apply_format consumes for a new (unrelated) search-api incident. 'Contributing factors (proposals)' carries punctuation in its label and its block ends in trailing spaces; 'Timeline' is a block whose label matches no heading in either entry, exercising the no-content-dropped append rule.

entries.content:
# 2026-07-21 — payments-api: partial outage

## What happened
Checkout returned 502s for roughly six minutes while the payments-api dependency was redeployed.

## Root cause (proposal)
The payments-api rollout skipped its health-check gate, so traffic shifted to instances that were still starting up.

## Contributing factors (proposals)
No canary stage existed for payments-api, and the on-call rotation had just handed off minutes before the alert fired.

## Action items (proposals)
Add a canary health-check gate before promoting payments-api releases.

# 2026-07-09 — checkout: brief overload

## What happened
Checkout queued requests for about four minutes during an unexpected traffic spike.

## Root cause (proposal)
An upstream marketing campaign drove a traffic spike the autoscaler had not seen modeled before.

## Contributing factors (proposals)
The autoscaler's warm-up window was tuned for gradual growth, not a step change.

## Action items (proposals)
Pre-warm checkout capacity ahead of known marketing campaigns.

sections: search-api exhausted its database connection pool for about seven minutes during a deploy.
The new search-api revision opened a fresh connection pool per worker instead of reusing the shared pool.
The connection pool size was never tuned down after search-api's worker count was doubled last quarter.
Cap the per-worker connection pool size and add an alert on pool exhaustion.
12:40 pool exhaustion began. 12:44 triage began. 12:47 the deploy was rolled back. 12:51 connections recovered.
